projects.clamp.title.text: Driving Sim Brake Controller Desk Clamp
projects.clamp.overview.title: site.hopen
Overview
site.hclose
projects.clamp.overview.1: A customer required a low-cost, small-footprint desk clamp on which to mount a peripheral device for a rally-car simulation
game. The clamp needed to withstand repeated application of force at a nominal 20Nm, whilst remaining easily removable and
relocatable. It was also required to avoid damage to the desk surface, with some consideration given to aesthetics.
projects.clamp.overview.3: "It has exceeded my expectations. I've moved from top 20% to top 9%".
projects.clamp.keydesign.title: site.hopen
Key Design Elements
site.hclose
projects.clamp.keydesign.1: Narrow, low-profile mounting plate for the peripheral device with a rubberised underside.
projects.clamp.keydesign.2: Large captive domed washers with rubber pads to distribute clamping force over a broad surface area.
projects.clamp.keydesign.3: Welded corners introduced to overcome mechanical failure observed during fabrication.
projects.clamp.keydesign.4: A makeshift forming jig was fashioned from scrap materials to permit accurate, repeatable formation of
domed clamp washers without deformation of the mating surface.
projects.clamp.gallery.title: site.hopen
Gaming Controller Desk Clamp
site.hclose
projects.clamp.demonstrates.title: site.hopen
What this demonstrates
site.hclose
projects.clamp.demonstrates.1: Application of the same design and fabrication principles employed in the Luggage Box project.
projects.clamp.demonstrates.2: Use of new and/or repurposed materials where warranted.
projects.clamp.demonstrates.3: Effective use of epoxy-based putty and repurposed bottle caps to maintain both functionality and aesthetics.
projects.clamp.demonstrates.4: Recognition of material limitations during fabrication: cold forging proved unsuitable, and mechanical stress induced
failure during formation of the second bend. In the end, welding provided the correct engineering solution.
projects.clamp.discussion.title: site.hopen
Discussion
site.hclose
projects.clamp.discussion.1: It was expected that scoring and cold forging of the steel strip would yield a work-hardened result that would not require welding.
This proved not to be an effective approach because the deformation of the initial bend resulted in mechanical fatigue and failure
of the metal. It was welded and the weld proved effective.
projects.clamp.discussion.2: This work was an exercise in prototyping as new approaches were taken to form the domed washers and clamp handles.
projects.clamp.discussion.3: Given materials and time constraints, commercial mudflap washers were chosen as suitable starting blanks from which to form the
clamping pads. In practice, the internal diameter was a little large, especially after the doming operation. It was envisaged that
the dome could be formed by tightening a 1/4" countersunk screw onto the washer, and mating it with a countersunk rebate in the die
surface of the jig. It was not possible to exert enough force so a piece of 1/2" steel rod with a 45 degree taper was used as a punch.
projects.clamp.discussion.4: Various options were considered for the handles for the clamp screws. A simple cross-bar was considered, as was cast aluminium.
A three or five lobed star knob seemed a nice option but forming that accurately, without a CNC cutter to either form it, or to
form a pattern for casting, quickly precluded that option. Kirk's drink bottles feature a nice, black lid design — and they
are readily available. The screw shanks were trimmed, and deeply keyed with a grinder. The lids were packed with ‘Kneadit’ epoxy
putty and pressed onto the keyed spindles whilst ensuring displaced material was worked back into the lids.
projects.clamp.discussion.5: A revision is being considered. If it is actualised, it will feature custom clamp-washers, more accurate seating of the washers on
the clamp screws, a narrower clamp section with an articulated platform for the peripheral device. A rivet will secure the front of
the platform to the clamp-body, and a curved slot will allow the back end to be adjusted so that it can be angled relative to the
clamp. A set-screw arrangement will secure the back end. Another option under consideration, albeit at the cost of additional
complexity, is a sliding mounting platform providing two degrees of freedom.
